item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Procedures make no sense whatsoever. Forget the Ethics Committee, the Foundation's basic fucking mission statement says researchers had no right to let conditions inside the farmhouse get this bad. "Oooooooooh noooooo, the SCP-Object can't figure out how to operate foundation technology! This makes sense!" Then you send in remote-controlled drones and a bunch of spare parts and you build a fucking incinerator inside the house and cut a hole in the ceiling if you have to! No more than one instance of SCP-2638-A is needed for study. Terminate the extras and incinerate the corpses.

That's what the Foundation is doing wrong here. Now let's talk about how Jacqui is holding the idiot ball. She claims she wants weapons. Why? Weapons would only be useful for two things: Killing the SCP-2638-A instances, or killing herself. If the SCP-2638-A instances continue talking after dead, the weapons are useless for that purpose. If they don't, Jacqui could easily murder them with a kitchen knife or a simple sharpened stick. She could commit suicide the same way, too.

I guess it sort of works as general-purpose creepypasta? But as a skip it lacks basic common sense on the parts of both the Foundation and the Contained. The only reason the story ends the way it does is because none of the people in the story are even trying to pursue their alleged goals.

A good skip is like good sci-fi— it gets better with Fridge Logic! This skip falls apart the moment you ask yourself "why."

item.title: Re: My first SCP
item.content:encoded: This is a very good first SCP, keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What doctor is delivering babies AND treating patients for depression?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think I've ever heard speech in writing anything quite as realistic and profoundly unsettling as:

Would you please just shut up and die already, Sharon.

Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, great SCP interview and is particularly original. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for the lovely comments everyone :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any interpretation is valid, of course, but in my head Brian did cause this. Unfortunately his deep psychological problems (e.g. fear of abandonment and ignorance of normal human interaction) created something he did not intend at all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The -As can't leave. I should have made that explicit perhaps.

The addenda are summaries of several seperate reports on different sources. Plus it makes it easier to read :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamn, there's something I can't quite put my finger on that I love about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this is pleasantly messed up. :) My question is, can the -A's leave the house if forced out? Did I miss that? Well, that and why are all the addenda separate? They read like paragraphs in the same report on the history of the house occupants.

SCP-2638-2: Would you please just shut up and die already, Sharon.

My favorite part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this may have been the result of something he did that didn't quite work. Or worked entirely too well

She is a noted extrovert, and there is the compulsion effect bringing 'visitors' around. And making them stay. Maybe something to assure she'd have company and wouldn't be lonely after he eventually offed himself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sartre's "No Exit" as rewritten by Tennessee Williams.

The wife's comment that her husband only wanted to make her happy made me think that this may have been the result of something he did that didn't quite work. Or worked entirely too well. The addendum referring to the 1926 article bolstered this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's beautiful. Made me tear up a bit.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great - I love skips that deal with people as people.It's got some rough edges, but really came together in addendums and the interview. Really great work, and congrats on doing so well with your first skip. I hope you stick around and we get to see you grow and continue to contribute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reminds me of Vetaro's SCP-2449, but in a good way. Having to make small talk for all eternity sounds like a living hell. Jesus. +1

ETA: Also, what if it doesn't end with the death of the couple? Is this just going to be a house full of ditzy Southern gossips wandering around and making inane small talk until the end of eternity? Why is this a sentence you've made me type?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Again, I really like the last two lines of the interview. Nicely done.

item.title: My first SCP
item.content:encoded: Thanks go to Zyn and Hyperfluid for their feedback :-)
